label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking what happens when they are looking for a missing child, according to the passage. The options are: (A) can always find him (B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above (C) just try one way or another (D) won't give up until they find him Now, looking at the passage: The passage describes various methods used to find a missing child: putting an APB (all points bulletin), calling hospitals, contacting parents, friends, or relatives, following movements, checking local or national papers, putting up posters, going on television, and a magazine with photos of missing children. But the passage also says: "Of course, with nearly two million missing children every year, we can't do all these things for everyone. We haven't got the time, or the money , or the people who work for it." This directly supports option (B) "couldn't always try every way mentioned above." The passage clearly states that they can't do all the methods for every missing child due to limited resources like time, money, and personnel. Let me check the other options to be sure: (A) "can always find him" - The passage doesn't say they can always find the child. In fact, the existence of two million missing children per year implies they don't always find them. (C) "just try one way or another" - The passage doesn't suggest they
